[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2026-03-12
[TITLE]: PHOTOS: The YWCA Greenwich 8th Annual Women Who Inspire Awards - Living Greenwich
[METADATA/INTRO]: Greenwich Country Club has hosted its share of memorable evenings, but on March 4th, the room felt different. By 5:30, the cocktail hour was already humming — the kind of energy that builds when people are genuinely glad to be...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Greenwich Country Club has hosted its share of memorable evenings, but on March 4th, the room felt different. By 5:30, the cocktail hour was already humming — the kind of energy that builds when people are genuinely glad to be somewhere. The occasion was the 8th Annual Women Who Inspire Awards, and YWCA Greenwich didn’t underdeliver. 
 Ten women were recognized with the 2026 Women Who Inspire Award: Sara Allard, Kate Clark, Alyssa Gillego MD, Erin Glasebrook, Laura Jordan, Rachel Khanna, Susan Reynolds Lehman, Cristin Marandino, Mimi Santry, and Sandra Wainwright MD. The awards recognize outstanding achievement across philanthropic, professional, and volunteer work — and this group reflected the full range of what that can look like.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: YWCA Women of the Year Include Several Concordia Connects | Concordia College
[METADATA/INTRO]: The YWCA Cass Clay honored women whose work and passion benefit our community at the 2019 Women of the Year event. Concordia had eight alumni and faculty nominated for the awards and six won in their respective categories. The following...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: “Danyel and I were so honored for Ladybosses of Fargo-Moorhead to be nominated for YWCA Women of the Year and totally blown away to leave with an award,” Caroon says. “This community is full of amazing women and we’re humbled to be recognized in this way.” 
 Photo of all 2019 recipients: 
 Back row: Susan Jarvis, Taya Spelhaug, Jana Bruhschwein, Britt Selbo, Danyel Moe, Laura Caroon, Dawn Duncan, Maureen Bartelt
